benchmark_report.test_scenario: Refactor a simple Java function to follow clean code principles
benchmark_report.methodology: Random selection of 5 commands with scientific analysis

commands_analyzed.name: adhd-context-switch.md
commands_analyzed.savage_commentary: 47 template variables for context switching. This isn't automation, it's bureaucracy with XML seasoning. The '50% reduction in switching overhead' claim lacks any supporting data.

commands_analyzed.name: ultrathink-hybrid-mcp.md
commands_analyzed.savage_commentary: 15 confidence checks for basic problem solving. Spends more time measuring confidence than building competence. 'Medium performance' should be labeled 'GLACIAL'.

commands_analyzed.name: adhd-task-breakdown.md
commands_analyzed.savage_commentary: Brilliant spiciness rating system ruined by 2.4x time inflation. Creates self-fulfilling prophecies of failure through pessimistic estimates.

commands_analyzed.name: git-backup-sync.md
commands_analyzed.savage_commentary: 531 lines for 'git pull && git push'. Your 'intelligent conflict resolution' is git mergetool with a philosophy degree. NASA mission control has fewer metrics.

commands_analyzed.name: reasoning-wrapper.md
commands_analyzed.savage_commentary: Least terrible of the bunch. Enhancement modes make sense, meta-enhancement is useful. Still 415 lines for a 50-line template generator.

scientific_conclusions.primary_finding: 80% of commands violate CLAUDE.md complexity standards
scientific_conclusions.reliability_assessment: Unacceptably high variance in success rates
scientific_conclusions.user_experience_impact: Commands require PhD in XML templating to understand

improvement_recommendations: Apply CLAUDE.md complexity limits ruthlessly - score < 5 mandatory
Eliminate template variable soup - max 5 variables per command
Replace XML orchestration with simple bash scripts
Measure actual vs claimed performance improvements
Delete everything and start with 20-line solutions

methodology_notes.complexity_scoring: Based on CLAUDE.md factors: classes, patterns, configs
methodology_notes.success_rate_estimation: Based on dependency count and complexity
